Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAIMIE DAVIS' Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award.

Moving party Jaimie Davis has filed a petition to vacate an arbitration award. The arbitration award was previously confirmed by this court and judgment was entered. Davis has appealed the judgment. The court therefore lacks jurisdiction to revisit the judgment; the perfecting of an appeal from an order or judgment divests this court of jurisdiction over matters embracing or affecting the appeal.

Davis appears to be making a distinction between Beugelmans and the other parties to the arbitration. Beugelmans is a party to the judgment, which has been appealed, and therefore the court lacks jurisdiction over claims related to the Beugelmans judgment.

Prposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice And Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAIMIE DAVIS' Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs.

Moving party Jaimie Davis seeks an order striking $639 in costs sought by Qian & Nemecek LLP and David P. Nemecek (collectively "Nemecek"). The motion is denied.

Davis moves on the ground that the arbitrator's determination of costs is conclusive and final, and the court may not add any costs the arbitrator has not awarded. Here, Nemecek's cost bill seeks litigation costs rather than costs incurred in arbitration. (See Cost Bill & Nemecek Decl., para. 6.) "A court must award costs in a judicial proceeding to confirm, correct or vacate an arbitration award." (Corona v. Amherst Partners (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 701, 707 [citing Code Civ. Proc. 1293.2; Carole Ring & Associates v. Nicastro (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 253].) The costs are therefore proper.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Jul-01-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff JAIMIE DAVIS (Tel: 714-598-5999) in pro per;
Atty. VANESSA H. HUBERT, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming, LLP (Tel: 213-623-7400) for defendants Beugelmans, LLP and David P. Nemecek, Jr.;
Atty. DAVID P. NEMECEK, Esq. of Qian & Nemecek, LLP (Tel: 415-894-5045) for defendants Qian & Nemecek LLP.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAIMIE DAVIS' Motion To Sever A Term From The Arbitration Agreement And To Appoint An Arbitrator Per Section 1281.6 Of The Code Of Civil Procedure.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to sever and appoint a new arbitrator is denied. First, it is dubious whether the court has jurisdiction to provide the remedy that plaintiff seeks. (See Optimal Markets, Inc. v. Salant (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 912, 923-924 [describing limits of the court's "vestigial jurisdiction" once arbitration is compelled].) Second, plaintiff's remedy is to front Qian's fee and seek re-allocation when the award is issued under the JAMS rules. (See JAMS Comprehensive Arbitration Rules & Procedures 6(c), 24(f), and 31(c).)

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR No. 10619, Email: raquel@transcriptlegal.com; Tel: 925-384-0003 (302/SRB)

PLAINTIFF JAIMIE DAVIS' Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt And For Sanctions For Violations Of The Court Order Compelling Participation In Arbitration As To Defendant Yunji Willa Qian.

Plaintiff's motion for an order to show cause is denied. Plaintiff says a party to an arbitration is violating a JAMS rule that each party pay a pro rata share of JAMS fees and expenses. "It is the job of the arbitrator, not the court, to resolve all questions needed to resolve the controversy." (Optimal Markets, Inc. v. Salant (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 912, 924.) As stated in this court's order sending this matter to arbitration, "[o]ther than those actions authorized by statute," the court has no power to act once an arbitration begins. (Id. at 923.) Plaintiff cites no statutory authority for the notion that a court is to enforce an arbitrator's fee-and-expense rule.

order is signed.
